content:
First we place the rectangle in the coordinate‐plane:

 A=(0,0), B=(5,0), C=(5,12), D=(0,12).

The condition “distances from E to AB and to AD are 12 and 1” forces E to lie on the lines y=±12 and x=±1.  Checking acuteness of ΔABE shows the only choice is 

 E=(1,−12).

We look for a line through E which meets AB at F=(f,0) and CD at G=(g,12) and cuts the 5×12 rectangle (area 60) into two area‐30 pieces.  Collinearity E–F–G gives

 slope EF = 12/(f−1), slope EG=24/(g−1),

so 12/(f−1)=24/(g−1) ⇒ g−1=2(f−1) ⇒ g=2f−1.

Now the piece F–B–C–G is a quadrilateral of area  
½| f·0 +5·12+5·12 +g·0  −(0·5 +0·5 +12·g +12·f)|  
= 60−6(f+g).  

Setting this =30 ⇒ 60−6(f+g)=30 ⇒ f+g=5.  Hence

 f+(2f−1)=5 ⇒3f=6 ⇒f=2,  g=3.

Thus F=(2,0), G=(3,12) and

 FG²=(3−2)²+(12−0)²=1+144=145.

Answer: \boxed{145}.

so 12/(f−1)=24/(g−1) ⇒ g−1=2(f−1) ⇒ g=2f−1.

Now the piece F–B–C–G is a quadrilateral of area

= 60−6(f+g).  

Setting this =30 ⇒ 60−6(f+g)=30 ⇒ f+g=5.  Hence

 f+(2f−1)=5 ⇒3f=6 ⇒f=2,  g=3.

 FG²=(3−2)²+(12−0)²=1+144=145.

Answer: \boxed{145}.
